page: Hetzner Cloud ccx53 is a dedicated CPU virtual server belonging to the ccx family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is configured with 125.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 3.91 GB of RAM per core, and includes 600 GB of local SSD storage. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included in the base configuration.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and bzip3 single-threaded decompression scoring in the top 25%. Cryptographic execution is also a strength, as evidenced by a strong PassMark CPU encryption test result. Memory performance is robust, with a PassMark memory score in the top 25%, though uncached large-block read and write memory bandwidths remain in the average tier at 84.57 GB/sec and 62.84 GB/sec respectively. Multi-core performance, including Geekbench multi-core and PassMark CPU scores, falls within the average range. For language model workloads, the server achieves top-tier performance in text generation for small 135M models, but drops to poor performance when executing text generation on large 70B models due to the lack of GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ccx53 offers a balanced resource density with dedicated CPU allocation, making it a viable option for workloads requiring predictable compute performance without the overhead of shared-core environments. The primary tradeoff is the absence of a GPU, which limits its utility for heavy deep learning or large-scale AI inference, as shown by the low tokens-per-second rate on 70B models. However, the balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio of nearly 1:4 makes it highly suitable for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, database operations, and medium-scale web serving.
description: Hetzner Cloud ccx53 is a dedicated virtual server powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor with 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and 125.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, this instance features 600 GB of local SSD storage. Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance, high cryptographic throughput, and top-tier text generation speeds for small language models. However, multi-core benchmarks, database operations, and large-block memory bandwidth fall into the average tier, while large-scale LLM text generation is poor due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware. With a balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio of approximately 1:4, the ccx53 provides a cost-effective, predictable environment for general-purpose applications, software compilation, and web serving.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx53 is a dedicated virtual server featuring 32 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 125.0 GB of RAM, and 600 GB of SSD storage, delivering strong single-core and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx53 is a dedicated x86_64 server with 32 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 125.0 GB RAM, and 600 GB SSD storage for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC server offering balanced compute and memory resources with strong single-core performance and local SSD storage.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs and 125.0 GB of RAM
600 GB of local SSD storage included
Strong single-core CPU and cryptographic benchmark performance
Top-tier text generation speeds for small-scale language models
Well-suited for software compilation, database operations, and web serving
